utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: judy gomez , mrn 869723 . date of birth , 5 , 7 , 1961 . she's in office today for ongoing management of psoriatic arthritis . hello , judy , how are you doing today ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i'm doing good , thank you . how are you ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i'm great , thanks . so how have you been since the last time ? i know the last time we were talking about decreasing your prednisone dose , correct ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes . i'm just on one now and that seems to be enough .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: aw , that's great to hear .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , there were a couple days there i took an extra one , just because there was a little extra pain in my feet . and i do have a desk job , so when i have a day off where i'm moving around a lot they do tend to hurt a bit more .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . how many times did you do that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: um , it was n't often , maybe once a week .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . so it sounds like we're still on track for discontinuing the prednisone . we'll do that today and you can let me know how it goes on your next visit . and how about the methotrexate , do you think that helped with your joint pain ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , definitely . because i went to get my covid shots , um , but from the letter i got about it , it said that it could interfere with the vaccine , you know , reduce the efficacy . so i did n't take it on the week that i got the first shot .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and what happened ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i felt absolutely horrible until i took it again a week later .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i'm sorry to hear that . and- and what did you do about the second dose ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: well , i called into the hotline because i was in so much pain with the first one . and they said , " no , just go ahead and take it , " so i did .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . that's good then . we do have to keep an eye out on it since it's a high-risk medication . do you have an appointment to get your blood drawn for the next time ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , they did n't give me one .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . so we can do that for you too . uhm , so what questions do you have for me , judy ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: well , i just wanted to know why i was getting all these bruises here , so like when i bump myself . i do n't know where they're coming from .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . that's probably from the prednisone , it can increase bruising .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay . i did n't know that . um , i do n't even feel it when it happens , they just show up .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah . unfortunately that can happen , but we're working on discontinuing that so let's see if the bruises do go away .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay . that sounds good , thanks .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , judy , please , um , sit up here and i'll take a look . shoes and socks off please .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: all right .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . let me see here . okay . so where is it hurting ? in your joints right here ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , a little . also in my feet joints as well .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and how about when you bend the knee like this ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: well , it hurt before we increased the methotrexate , but it's doing pretty good now .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , good . can you flex your toes please ? good range of motion . also ridges in nails , that's from the psoriasis .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah . they've been like that for a long time now .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right , uhm , ms gomez , it looks like we're moving along with your treatment nicely . we'll stop your prednisone and continue with the methotrexate . make sure to stop by the front desk and make an appointment for the blood work , and i'll see you in three months .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: all right . it sounds good . thank you so much , it was great to see you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: it was great seeing you too . thank you .

detail_list.summary: Judy Gomez is a 61-year-old female presenting for management of psoriatic arthritis.
Psoriatic arthritis
The doctor asks about the patient's progress regarding the reduction of prednisone.
The patient is currently taking 1 mg of prednisone daily.
The patient reports taking 2 mg of prednisone for a few days due to increased foot pain.
The doctor asks how frequently the patient increased her prednisone dose.
The patient increased her prednisone dose approximately once a week.
The doctor confirms the plan to discontinue prednisone.
The doctor asks if the patient believes methotrexate has helped with her joint pain.
The patient reports methotrexate helps her pain, but she held the dose for her first COVID-19 vaccine due to concerns about efficacy.
The doctor asks what happened when the patient held her methotrexate dose.
The patient felt horrible after holding her methotrexate dose until she resumed it a week later.
The doctor asks how the patient managed her methotrexate for the second COVID-19 vaccine dose.
The patient did not hold her methotrexate for the second COVID-19 vaccine after consulting a hotline.
The doctor asks if the patient has an appointment scheduled for blood work.
The patient does not have an appointment for blood work.
The doctor asks if the patient has any questions.
The patient asks about the cause of her bruising.
The doctor explains that prednisone can cause increased bruising.
The doctor initiates a physical examination.
The doctor asks the patient to identify the location of her joint pain.
The patient reports pain in her joints and feet.
The doctor asks about pain during knee flexion.
The patient reports her knee pain has improved since the methotrexate dose was increased.
The patient demonstrates full range of motion in her toes, and the doctor notes nail ridges consistent with psoriasis.
The doctor instructs the patient to discontinue prednisone, continue methotrexate, schedule blood work, and return in three months.
